    <p>{`MetaFox platform follows `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/packages#service-providers"

    <p>{`Package directory structure`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "structure"
    }}>{`Structure`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`packages`}</inlineCode>{` directory contains all packages, organize as bellow convention.`}</p>
    <p>{`vendor_name should be your company name to avoid duplicating with others.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`packages/
    [company]/
        package-1/
        package-2/
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`In this section we explain detail for package directory structure.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`composer.json                : Package infomration and composer dependencies
config/                      :
  config.php                 : Contain package configuration
resources/                   :
  langs/
    en/                      : Language file for \`en\` locale
      phrase.php             : Define phrases for group \`phrase\`
      validator.php          : Define phrases for group \`validator\`
  menu/                      :
    menus.php                :
    menuitems.php            :
  routes/                    :
     api.php                 : Define restful api routes
     web.php                 : Define web routes
  src/
    Contracts/               : Contains basic interface defination
    Database/                :
      Factories              : Contains database model [factories](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/database-testing#defining-model-factories)
      Migrations             : Contains database [migrations](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/migrations#main-content)
      Seeders                : Contain database [seeders](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/seeding#introduction)
        PackageSeeder.php    : Entry pont for database seeder
  Http/
    Controllers/             :
    Requests/                :
    Resources/               :
  Jobs/                      :
  Listeners/                 :
  Mail/                      :
  Notification/              :
  Models/                    :
  Observers/                 :
  Policies/                  :
  Providers/                 :
  Repositories/              :
  Rules/                     :
  tests/                     :
    Features/                :
    Unit/                    :
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "composerjson"
    }}>{`Composer.json`}</h2>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "name": "metafox/blog",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Allows user posting blogs for MetaFox platform.",
  "authors": [
    {
      "name": "MetaFox",
      "email": "dev@phpfox.com",
      "homepage": "https://www.phpfox.com/"
    }
  ],
  "extra": {
    "metafox": {
      "path": "packages/metafox/blog",
      "nameAlias": "blog",
      "nameStudly": "Blog",
      "namespace": "MetaFox\\\\Blog",
      "internalUrl": "/blog",
      "internalAdminUrl": "/admincp/setting/edit/blog",
      "providers": ["MetaFox\\\\Blog\\\\Providers\\\\BlogServiceProvider"],
      "aliases": {}
    }
  },
  "require": {},
  "require-dev": {},
  "autoload": {
    "psr-4": {
      "MetaFox\\\\Blog\\\\": "src/"
    }
  },
  "autoload-dev": {
    "psr-4": {
      "MetaFox\\\\Blog\\\\Tests\\\\": "tests/"
    }
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`name`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The name of the package. It consists of vendor name and project name, separated by /. Examples:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`metafox/platform`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`metafox/blog`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`The name must be lowercase and consist of words separated by -, . or `}<em parentName="p">{`. The complete name should match ^`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`([`}</em>{`.-]?`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`+)`}<em parentName="p">{`/`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`(<([`}</em>{`.]?|-{0,2})`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`+>)`}{`_`}{`\\$.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`description`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`A short description of the package. Usually this is one line long.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{` version `}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The version of the package. In most cases this is not required and should be omitted (see below).`}</p>
    <p>{`For example, valid version`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`1.0.0`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`1.0.2`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`1.1.0`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`0.2.5`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`1.0.0-dev`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`1.0.0-alpha3`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`1.0.0-beta2`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`author.`}{`*`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The authors of the package`}</p>
    <p>{`An author object can have following properties:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`name: The author's name. Usually their real name.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`email: The author's email address.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`homepage: URL to the author's website.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`require`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Map of packages required by this package. The package will not be installed unless those requirements can be met.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`require-dev`}</strong>{`
Map of packages required for developing this package, or running tests, etc. The dev requirements of the root package are installed by default. Both install or update support the --no-dev option that prevents dev dependencies from being installed.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`autoload.psr-4`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Under the psr-4 key you define a mapping from namespaces to paths, relative to the package root. When autoloading a class like Foo`}{`\\`}{`Bar`}{`\\`}{`Baz a namespace prefix Foo`}{`\\`}{` pointing to a directory src/ means that the autoloader will look for a file named src/Bar/Baz.php and include it if present. Note that as opposed to the older PSR-0 style, the prefix (Foo`}{`\\`}{`) is not present in the file path.`}</p>
    <p>{`Namespace prefixes must end in `}{`\\`}{` to avoid conflicts between similar prefixes. For example Foo would match classes in the FooBar namespace so the trailing backslashes solve the problem: Foo`}{`\\`}{` and FooBar`}{`\\`}{` are distinct.`}</p>
    <p>{`The PSR-4 references are all combined, during install/update, into a single key => value array which may be found in the generated file vendor/composer/autoload_psr4.php.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`autoload.files`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`If you want to require certain files explicitly on every request then you can use the files autoloading mechanism. This is useful if your package includes PHP functions that cannot be autoloaded by PHP.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "autoload": {
    "files": ["src/MyLibrary/functions.php"]
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`autoload-dev`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`This section allows defining autoload rules for development purposes.`}</p>
    <p>{`Classes needed to run the test suite should not be included in the main autoload rules to avoid polluting the autoloader in production and when other people use your package as a dependency.`}</p>
    <p>{`Therefore, it is a good idea to rely on a dedicated path for your unit tests and to add it within the autoload-dev section.`}</p>
    <p>{`Example:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "autoload": {
    "psr-4": { "MyLibrary\\\\": "src/" }
  },
  "autoload-dev": {
    "psr-4": { "MyLibrary\\\\Tests\\\\": "tests/" }
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`extra.metafox.path`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The package source under root application.`}</p>
    <p>{`For example `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`packages/metafox/blog`}</inlineCode></p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`extra.metafox.alias`}</strong></p>

    <p><strong parentName="p">{`extra.metafox.namespace`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The root namespace of the package.`}</p>
    <p>{`It must not be trailing with "`}{`\\`}{`". For example, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\\\Blog`}</inlineCode></p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`extra.metafox.internalUrl`}</strong></p>
